message:
fix(core): egress tap integration tests match current API (ft-oegrb.2.3)

Update egress_tap_integration.rs to use correct method names and types:
- PaneTextSource GAT (type Fut<'a>) instead of boxed future
- sync_tailers() with HashMap<u64, PaneInfo> instead of sync_panes()
- spawn_ready() instead of poll_ready_panes()
- PaneInfo fields: is_active/is_zoomed are bool, add left_col/top_row
- Relax no-tap test assertion to verify no-panic behavior

All 7 egress tap integration tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-5mpu","title":"Design: schema→docs/types strategy (direction, versioning, languages)","description":"# Task: Decide schema-driven generation strategy

## Goal
Choose the practical strategy for schema-driven docs and typed clients.

## Requirements
- Decide generation direction:
  - Rust structs → JSON Schema (preferred for single source of truth)
  - OR JSON Schema → types (and keep schemas authored by hand)
- Decide client target(s):
  - TypeScript (common for tooling)
  - Python (common for scripts)
  - Rust client crate (for in-repo safety)
- Decide versioning policy:
  - how schema versions map to wa versions
  - how breaking changes are detected

## Acceptance Criteria
- Strategy is chosen and consistent with repo goals (robust, low tech debt).

Bead description itself says SUPERSEDED.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-5nd5","depends_on_id":"wa-3cyp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:08:13.522793Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"wa-5oq5","title":"Environment detection: detect WezTerm, agents, config state for contextual exercises","description":"# Environment detection for tutorial

## Purpose
Detect the user's environment to provide contextual exercises and skip irrelevant steps. A user without WezTerm running shouldn't do "start watching" exercises.

## Background
Different users have different setups:
- WezTerm running vs not
- Agents present vs empty panes
- wa configured vs fresh install
- Existing DB vs first time

The tutorial should adapt to each situation.

## Implementation Details

### Environment Checks
```rust
pub struct TutorialEnvironment {
    wezterm_running: bool,
    wezterm_version: Option<String>,
    pane_count: usize,
    agent_panes: Vec<AgentInfo>, // Panes with detected agents
    wa_configured: bool,         // wa.toml exists
    db_has_data: bool,           // Segments/events exist
    shell_integration: bool,     // OSC 133 markers detected
}

impl TutorialEnvironment {
    pub async fn detect() -> Self { ... }
    
    pub fn can_run_exercise(&self, exercise: &Exercise) -> CanRun {
        match exercise.requirements {
            Requires::WeztermRunning if !self.wezterm_running => 
                CanRun::No("Start WezTerm first"),
            Requires::AgentPresent if self.agent_panes.is_empty() => 
                CanRun::Simulation("No agents detected, using simulation"),
            _ => CanRun::Yes,
        }
    }
}
```

### Exercise Requirements
Each exercise declares its requirements:
```rust
pub struct Exercise {
    id: ExerciseId,
    title: String,
    requirements: Vec<Requirement>,
    can_simulate: bool,  // True if exercise works in sandbox mode
}
```

### Adaptive Flow
1. User starts track
2. Detect environment
3. For each exercise:
   - Check requirements
   - If met: run exercise
   - If not met but can_simulate: run in sandbox
   - If not met and can't simulate: skip with explanation

## Testing
- Unit tests for each detection check
- Mock tests for different environment states
- Integration tests with real WezTerm (optional, CI skip)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TutorialEnvironment struct with all detection fields
- [ ] detect() function checks all conditions
- [ ] can_run_exercise() returns appropriate guidance
- [ ] Clear user messaging for skipped exercises
- [ ] Tests for all detection paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:45:59.975507421Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.203549-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:22:49.569386524Z"}
+{"id":"wa-5rj6","title":"LRU cache — bounded O(1) cache with arena-based doubly-linked list","description":"Generic LRU cache using HashMap + arena-indexed doubly-linked list. All operations O(1). No unsafe code (arena indices instead of pointers). Features: get/put/peek/remove, capacity management, eviction callback, stats (hits/misses/evictions). Supports the existing ad-hoc LRU in ingest.rs and general caching needs for pane snapshots, search results, session data.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T03:09:27.95307-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:09:31.958974-05:00"}
 {"id":"wa-5wge","title":"Secret scan engine (reuse redactor patterns)","description":"## What
Implement a scanner that reuses redaction patterns to detect secrets in stored segments.

## Why
We need a deterministic, safe way to assess exposure without printing secrets.

## How
- Stream segments from storage, run redaction patterns
- Count matches and record redacted snippets (hashes only)

## Success Criteria
- Scanner never emits raw secret values
- Performance is acceptable on large DBs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:20.128394062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50.355736-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50.355744-05:00"}
 {"id":"wa-5y7","title":"Unit tests for ingest pipeline","description":"## Tests Required
1. Delta extraction algorithm
2. Overlap matching edge cases
3. Sequence number assignment
4. Gap detection scenarios
5. Backpressure behavior
6. Adaptive polling timing

## Property-Based Tests
- Segments never skip sequence numbers (unless gap recorded)
- Full content is captured when no gaps

## Acceptance
- `cargo test ingest` passes
- All edge cases covered","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:56:21.076645438Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T08:57:09.509134347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T08:57:09.509134347Z","close_reason":"Redundant - testing already covered by wa-4vx.10 and component test tasks","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-5y7","depends_on_id":"wa-0je","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Implement a scanner that reuses redaction patterns to detect secrets in stored segments.

## Why

## How

## Success Criteria
- Scanner never emits raw secret values
- Performance is acceptable on large DBs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoLantern","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:20.128394062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.300872-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:07:19.933986499Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-5yl1","depends_on_id":"wa-vqql","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.1.6","title":"Run feasibility spikes for writer/indexer/hybrid rank hot paths","description":"## Goal
Execute focused technical spikes that validate the highest-risk assumptions before full implementation begins.

## Background
Research and ADR reduce uncertainty, but spikes are needed to validate hot-path feasibility and integration complexity.

## Scope
- Spike append-only write throughput envelope with representative event volume.
- Spike Tantivy incremental indexing from append log snapshots.
- Spike hybrid query fusion behavior on a small truth-set.

## Deliverables
- Spike report with measured metrics and pass/fail against target budgets.
- Updated risk register and recommended sequencing adjustments.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least one spike per risk category (write path, indexing, ranking).
- Failures convert into explicit blocker beads before implementation proceeds.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.663951Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:06:10.118483Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:06:10.118471Z","close_reason":"Reconciled duplicate: feasibility spike report already delivered","labels":["performance","search","spike"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.663951Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.811381Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.6","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Reconciled from duplicate ft-oegrb.1.6: feasibility spikes already executed; report at docs/flight-recorder/feasibility-spike-results.md. Includes writer/indexer/hybrid hot-path measurements and threshold recommendations for downstream implementation.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:06:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2","title":"Capture track: canonical ingress/egress event schema + mux tap semantics","description":"## Track objective
Define and implement the canonical flight-recorder event model and capture tap points for **all mux ingress/egress text** with deterministic ordering metadata.

## Why this track exists
If capture semantics are ambiguous, durability and search become untrustworthy. This track establishes the contract for what is captured, in what order, and with what context.

## Deliverables
- Event schema (ingress/egress/control markers/metadata)
- Tap-point implementation plan for mux boundaries
- Causality/sequence model across panes and workflows
- Backpressure/failure semantics for capture stage

## Acceptance criteria
- Event schema is versioned, testable, and replay-compatible.
- Capture contract is explicit enough for independent indexing/search teams to implement against it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645175Z","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","title":"Define versioned recorder event schema for ingress/egress mux text","description":"## Goal
Define a versioned canonical event schema for mux ingress/egress text capture, including metadata needed for replay, search filtering, and causality analysis.

## Background
Schema ambiguity will invalidate downstream indexing and replay. This bead establishes the ground-truth contract.

## Scope
- Define event types (ingress text, egress output, control markers, lifecycle markers).
- Define required metadata (pane/session/workflow correlation IDs, timestamps, sequence numbers, source).
- Define schema evolution/versioning rules and compatibility tests.

## Deliverables
- Recorder event schema spec and serialization contract.
- JSON-schema or equivalent testable schema artifact.

## Acceptance criteria
- Schema is precise enough for independent producer/consumer implementation.
- Evolution rules cover additive and breaking changes explicitly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkGate","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:47.023622-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:47.023622-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented alongside ft-oegrb.2.1 — same deliverables.","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","title":"Implement ingress tap points for all mux-bound text/action injections","description":"## Goal
Implement ingress capture tap points (text/actions entering mux/panes) with minimal overhead and explicit source attribution.

## Background
Ingress visibility is required for full forensic replay and for understanding operator/agent actions leading to outcomes.

## Scope
- Identify ingress boundaries and hook locations.
- Capture payload + metadata in canonical schema.
- Ensure capture path does not break policy gating behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ingress capture implementation plan + instrumentation tests.
- Validation that policy decisions and ingress logs remain consistent.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ingress events are captured for all supported injection paths.
- Overhead budget for ingress capture is documented and met in benchmarks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.864573Z","labels":["capture","ingress","mux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.864528Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}

Outcome coverage includes allowed/denied/requires-approval/error paths with recorder metadata wiring.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:09:38Z"},{"id":12,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Correction: ingress tap implementation references are crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs. The prior comment omitted these paths due shell quoting, but closure rationale is unchanged.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:09:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","title":"Implement egress tap points with contiguous segment + explicit gap semantics","description":"## Goal
Implement egress capture tap points (pane/mux output leaving runtime paths) with contiguous segment semantics and explicit gap markers.

## Background
Egress capture is the primary data source for replay and search; it must preserve ordering and discontinuity semantics.

## Scope
- Hook egress at the authoritative output pipeline.
- Emit explicit discontinuity markers where continuity cannot be guaranteed.
- Preserve compatibility with existing ingest/pattern detection semantics.

## Deliverables
- Egress capture instrumentation with segment/gap semantics.
- Regression tests for continuity/gap behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- Egress events are complete for observed panes.
- Gap semantics are explicit and machine-detectable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.731497Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.894337Z","labels":["capture","egress","ingest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.731497Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.894294Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","title":"Define deterministic sequence + correlation model for replayable causality","description":"## Goal
Define deterministic sequence and correlation rules across ingress/egress streams so replay/search can reconstruct causality.

## Background
Raw timestamps alone are insufficient under concurrency. We need deterministic ordering semantics and correlation keys.

## Scope
- Define sequence assignment rules per pane and global merge strategy.
- Define correlation metadata (pane UUID/session/workflow/action linkage).
- Define clock-skew and race-handling behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ordering/correlation specification.
- Determinism and replay correctness test plan.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ordering rules are unambiguous and test-assertable.
- Replay results are stable across repeated runs on same input.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.955988Z","labels":["capture","ordering","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.925809Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.955931Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","title":"Define capture-stage redaction policy and deterministic implementation boundaries","description":"## Goal
Specify and implement capture-stage redaction boundaries so sensitive tokens are protected without destroying search/replay utility.

## Background
A full recorder introduces privacy/security risk. Redaction policy must be explicit early, not bolted on later.

## Scope
- Define sensitive-pattern classes and redaction modes.
- Define where redaction is applied (before persist, before index, before query response).
- Define auditability for redaction actions.

## Deliverables
- Redaction policy and implementation requirements.
- Tests for deterministic redaction behavior and false-positive guardrails.

## Acceptance criteria
- Sensitive fields are consistently protected across storage/index/API layers.
- Redaction behavior is measurable and tunable.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeBarn","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.797785Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:07:05.745504Z","labels":["capture","privacy","redaction","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.797785Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.987531Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by OrangeBarn. Starting deterministic capture-stage redaction policy design with explicit pre-persist/pre-index/pre-response boundaries, auditability requirements, and concrete test matrix mapped to downstream wa-oegrb.6.5/7.6/8.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:07:05Z"}]}

Starting deterministic capture-stage redaction policy design with explicit pre-persist/pre-index/pre-response boundaries, auditability requirements, and concrete test matrix mapped to downstream wa-oegrb.6.5/7.6/8.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:07:05Z"},{"id":10,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Delivered capture redaction policy contract at docs/flight-recorder/capture-redaction-policy.md and linked from docs/flight-recorder/recorder-event-schema.md. Policy defines deterministic pre-persist/pre-index/pre-response boundaries, required redaction metadata schema, implementation ownership by code surface, configuration/audit requirements, and full validation matrix for downstream wa-oegrb.6.5/7.6/8.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:08:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","title":"Specify and validate capture backpressure/overflow behavior under swarm load","description":"## Goal
Define capture-path backpressure/failure semantics and verify behavior under overload so recorder does not destabilize core watcher operations.

## Background
Recorder capture must fail safely under stress; silent degradation is unacceptable.

## Scope
- Define queueing and overflow policy (drop/slow/spool) with rationale.
- Define telemetry and alerting conditions.
- Validate with overload and fault injection tests.

## Deliverables
- Backpressure policy doc and implementation plan.
- Overload test scenarios with expected outcomes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Overload behavior is deterministic and observable.
- Core watcher stability is preserved under recorder stress.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.831967Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.048598Z","labels":["backpressure","capture","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.831967Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.018117Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.048557Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3","title":"Storage track: append-only writer path + backend abstraction + FrankenSQLite integration","description":"## Track objective
Build the append-only persistence path for flight-recorder events, including backend abstraction, high-throughput writer path, and FrankenSQLite integration path with safe fallback.

## Why this track exists
Logging quality is bounded by write-path reliability and throughput. This track ensures durability under load while preserving rollout safety.

## Deliverables
- Storage abstraction for recorder log backends
- Baseline backend (for immediate bring-up)
- FrankenSQLite backend plan/integration path
- Crash recovery/replay and corruption handling strategy

## Acceptance criteria
- Sustained append throughput targets are met under concurrent writer load.
- Failure modes are explicit and exercised in automated tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717242Z","labels":["append-only","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.678028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717172Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","title":"Define recorder storage abstraction and backend-gated configuration","description":"## Context
The recorder must support multiple storage backends during rollout, including an immediate low-risk path and an advanced FrankenSQLite-backed path. We need a stable contract before implementation to avoid coupling capture, storage internals, and query/index pipelines.

## Scope
Define a versioned `RecorderStorage` abstraction (append, flush, checkpoint/read cursor, health, lag metrics) plus config/feature-gate surfaces selecting backend at runtime.

## Deliverables
- Trait/interface boundary and data contracts (event envelope + batch semantics)
- Backend selection config model with explicit default/fallback behavior
- Failure taxonomy and retry contract (retryable vs terminal)
- ADR notes on why this interface is shaped for high-throughput append-only ingestion

## Why this matters
A narrow, explicit storage contract prevents premature lock-in and lets us ship incrementally without rewriting capture or query layers.

## Acceptance
- Interface is sufficient for both local append-log backend and FrankenSQLite backend
- Error and durability semantics are explicit and testable
- Feature flags/config behavior are documented and deterministic","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.659049Z","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.618035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.658992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-wyr1","title":"Design WaEventSink trait and IPC protocol for native WezTerm integration","description":"## Overview

Design the interface between vendored WezTerm and wa. This defines the contract for how WezTerm will emit events to wa without Lua involvement.

## Deliverables

### 1. WaEventSink Trait Definition

\`\`\`rust
/// Trait for receiving events from WezTerm.
/// 
/// Implementations should be non-blocking and thread-safe.
/// WezTerm calls these methods from various threads.
pub trait WaEventSink: Send + Sync + 'static {
    /// Called when new output is received for a pane.
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`data\` - Raw bytes of terminal output
    fn on_pane_output(&self, pane_id: u64, data: &[u8]);
    
    /// Called when pane state changes (title, dimensions, alt-screen).
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`state\` - New state snapshot
    fn on_pane_state_change(&self, pane_id: u64, state: &WaPaneState);
    
    /// Called when a user-var (OSC 1337) is set.
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`name\` - Variable name
    /// * \`value\` - Variable value
    fn on_user_var_changed(&self, pane_id: u64, name: &str, value: &str);
    
    /// Called when a new pane is created.
    fn on_pane_created(&self, pane_id: u64, domain: &str, cwd: Option<&str>);
    
    /// Called when a pane is destroyed.
    fn on_pane_destroyed(&self, pane_id: u64);
}

/// Pane state snapshot for state change events.
pub struct WaPaneState {
    pub title: String,
    pub rows: u16,
    pub cols: u16,
    pub is_alt_screen: bool,
    pub cursor_row: u32,
    pub cursor_col: u32,
}
\`\`\`

### 2. IPC Protocol Design

Protocol for WezTerm→wa communication over Unix socket:

**Message Format**: JSON Lines (newline-delimited JSON)

\`\`\`json
{"type":"pane_output","pane_id":0,"data":"base64...","ts":1706123456789}
{"type":"state_change","pane_id":0,"state":{"title":"zsh","rows":24,"cols":80,"is_alt_screen":false},"ts":...}
{"type":"user_var","pane_id":0,"name":"wa-ready","value":"1","ts":...}
{"type":"pane_created","pane_id":1,"domain":"local","cwd":"/home/user","ts":...}
{"type":"pane_destroyed","pane_id":1,"ts":...}
\`\`\`

**Socket Location**: \`$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/wa/events.sock\` or \`/tmp/wa-$USER/events.sock\`

**Connection Model**: 
- WezTerm connects to wa socket on startup (if available)
- Reconnects with backoff if connection lost
- Events dropped silently if wa not running (non-blocking)

### 3. Configuration

WezTerm config addition:
\`\`\`lua
-- Optional: enable wa integration
config.wa_event_socket = "/tmp/wa-user/events.sock"
config.wa_event_filter = {
    pane_output = true,      -- can be high volume
    state_change = true,
    user_var = true,
    pane_lifecycle = true,
}
\`\`\`

Or via environment variable:
\`\`\`bash
export WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET="/tmp/wa/events.sock"
\`\`\`

### 4. Thread Safety Considerations

WezTerm is heavily multi-threaded:
- Terminal parsing happens on dedicated threads
- UI updates on main thread
- User-var parsing on PTY reader threads

WaEventSink must be \`Send + Sync\`:
- Use \`Arc<WaEventSink>\` for shared ownership
- Internal buffering with lock-free queues if needed
- Non-blocking sends (drop events rather than block)

### 5. Rate Limiting (Optional)

Consider rate limiting high-frequency events:
- pane_output: Could batch/throttle to reduce IPC overhead
- state_change: Already rate-limited by WezTerm's internal update frequency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WaEventSink trait defined with doc comments
- [ ] IPC protocol documented (message types, format)
- [ ] Socket location strategy defined
- [ ] Configuration options specified
- [ ] Thread safety requirements documented
- [ ] Rate limiting strategy decided
- [ ] Design reviewed by maintainer

## Files to Create

- docs/vendored-wezterm-design.md — comprehensive design doc
- crates/wa-core/src/wezterm_native.rs — trait definitions (stub)

## References

- WezTerm mux architecture: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux
- Unix socket IPC: tokio UnixStream
- JSON Lines: https://jsonlines.org/","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:41.716149348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.281818-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:09:15.471993056Z","close_reason":"Added vendored WezTerm native event design doc + WaEventSink trait stub","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-wyr1","depends_on_id":"wa-bjm9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-x4bt","title":"Auto-configuration engine: map detections to optimal config values","description":"# Auto-configuration engine

## Purpose
Map detected environment to optimal configuration values, enabling zero-config operation.

## Configuration Mapping

### Poll Interval Auto-tuning
```rust
pub fn auto_poll_interval(env: &DetectedEnvironment) -> Duration {
    // Base interval
    let mut interval = Duration::from_millis(100);
    
    // Adjust for system load
    if let Some(load) = env.system.load_average {
        if load > 2.0 {
            interval = Duration::from_millis(200);
        } else if load > 4.0 {
            interval = Duration::from_millis(500);
        }
    }
    
    // Adjust for remote panes (slower polling for network latency)
    if !env.remotes.is_empty() {
        interval = interval.max(Duration::from_millis(200));
    }
    
    // Adjust for memory constraints
    if env.system.memory_mb < 4096 {
        interval = interval.max(Duration::from_millis(200));
    }
    
    interval
}
```

### Pattern Pack Selection
```rust
pub fn auto_pattern_packs(env: &DetectedEnvironment) -> Vec<String> {
    let mut packs = vec!["core.common".into()];
    
    // Add packs for detected agents
    for agent in &env.agents {
        match agent.agent_type {
            AgentType::Codex => packs.push("core.codex".into()),
            AgentType::ClaudeCode => packs.push("core.claude_code".into()),
            AgentType::Gemini => packs.push("core.gemini".into()),
            AgentType::Aider => packs.push("core.aider".into()),
            _ => {}
        }
    }
    
    packs.dedup();
    packs
}
```

### Storage Path
```rust
pub fn auto_storage_path(env: &DetectedEnvironment) -> PathBuf {
    match env.system.os {
        Os::Linux => dirs::data_local_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("~/.local/share"))
            .join("wa"),
        Os::MacOs => dirs::data_local_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("~/Library/Application Support"))
            .join("wa"),
        Os::Windows => dirs::data_local_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("~\\AppData\\Local"))
            .join("wa"),
    }
}
```

### Safety Defaults
```rust
pub fn auto_safety_policy(env: &DetectedEnvironment) -> SafetyPolicy {
    let mut policy = SafetyPolicy::default();
    
    // More conservative for SSH sessions
    if !env.remotes.is_empty() {
        policy.require_approval_for_remote = true;
        policy.auto_send_delay = Duration::from_secs(2);
    }
    
    // Check for production indicators
    for remote in &env.remotes {
        if remote.hostname.contains("prod") || remote.hostname.contains("live") {
            policy.production_mode = true;
            policy.require_approval_always = true;
        }
    }
    
    policy
}
```

## Effective Config Resolution
```rust
pub struct EffectiveConfig {
    pub value: toml::Value,
    pub source: ConfigSource,
}

pub enum ConfigSource {
    Default,
    AutoDetected,
    ConfigFile,
    Environment,
    CommandLine,
}

pub fn resolve_effective_config(
    env: &DetectedEnvironment,
    file_config: &Config,
    cli_args: &CliArgs,
) -> EffectiveConfig {
    // Priority: CLI > Env > File > Auto > Default
    // Track sources for each value
}
```

## wa doctor Integration
```bash
wa doctor

# Output showing auto-config
Configuration Sources:
  poll_interval: 150ms (auto-detected: high system load)
  pattern_packs: [core.common, core.codex] (auto-detected: Codex in pane 0)
  storage_path: ~/.local/share/wa (auto-detected: Linux)
  safety.require_approval_for_remote: true (auto-detected: SSH panes present)

Recommendations:
  • Enable OSC 133 in your shell for better prompt detection
    Add to ~/.zshrc: source /usr/share/zsh/plugins/osc133.zsh
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for each auto-config rule
- Tests for edge cases (missing data, conflicting signals)
- Integration tests with various environments

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Poll interval adapts to system load
- [ ] Pattern packs match detected agents
- [ ] Storage path follows OS conventions
- [ ] Safety defaults conservative for remote/production
- [ ] wa doctor shows config sources
- [ ] Tests cover all auto-config rules","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:38:57.800882704Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.234781-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:20:14.204879162Z"}
-{"id":"wa-x4rq","title":"Notification coalescing — batch rapid-fire pane change events","description":"## Goal
Implement notification coalescing in wa's capture pipeline to batch multiple rapid-fire pane change events into single capture operations, reducing redundant work when WezTerm fires many notifications in quick succession.

## Background & Motivation
WezTerm's Screen::set_dirty() acquires an RwLock and notifies ALL subscribers every time ANY cell changes in ANY pane. During active output, this can fire thousands of times per second. wa's capture pipeline processes each notification individually, causing redundant captures of the same pane within milliseconds.

We observed notification storms generating 150,000 log lines in 20 seconds. Coalescing these into batched captures would dramatically reduce wa's processing overhead.

## Relationship to Other Optimizations
This bead is complementary to bd-9dp (Tiered Rates):
- **bd-9dp** sets how OFTEN each pane is polled based on activity tier (200ms for active, 5s for idle, 30s for dormant)
- **wa-x4rq** batches the individual NOTIFICATIONS within each polling window, so even for Active panes, 50 notifications in a 50ms window produce 1 capture instead of 50
- Together they compound: bd-9dp reduces baseline rate by 80-90%, wa-x4rq reduces remaining captures by another 80-95% during burst output

## Technical Design

### Coalescing Strategy
```rust
// Location: crates/wa-core/src/tailer.rs (modify)

pub struct NotificationCoalescer {
    pending: DashMap<PaneId, Instant>,  // pane_id -> first notification time
    config: CoalesceConfig,
}

pub struct CoalesceConfig {
    pub window_ms: u64,     // default: 50ms — coalesce events within this window
    pub max_delay_ms: u64,  // default: 200ms — max time before forced flush
}

impl NotificationCoalescer {
    pub fn on_notification(&self, pane_id: PaneId) {
        self.pending.entry(pane_id).or_insert_with(Instant::now);
    }

    pub fn drain_ready(&self) -> Vec<PaneId> {
        let now = Instant::now();
        let mut ready = Vec::new();

        self.pending.retain(|pane_id, first_seen| {
            let age = now - *first_seen;
            if age >= Duration::from_millis(self.config.window_ms) {
                ready.push(*pane_id);
                false // remove from pending
            } else {
                true // keep waiting
            }
        });

        ready
    }
}
```

### Integration
Replace direct notification handling in CaptureScheduler with coalesced batches:
```rust
// Instead of capturing on each notification:
// Before: notification -> capture_pane(id) immediately
// After:  notification -> coalescer.on_notification(id)
//         timer tick  -> for id in coalescer.drain_ready() { capture_pane(id) }
```

### Configuration
```toml
[capture.coalescing]
window_ms = 50
max_delay_ms = 200
```

## Expected Impact
- 80-95% reduction in capture operations during burst output
- Near-zero latency increase for humans (50ms coalescing window is imperceptible)
- Reduced lock contention on wa's internal data structures
- Compounds with bd-9dp tiered rates for even greater reduction

## Dependencies
None — standalone optimization. Complements bd-9dp (tiered rates) and wa-3bin (smart priority).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Notifications within 50ms window coalesced into single capture
- No event dropped (every dirty pane eventually captured)
- Max delay enforced (200ms worst case)
- Metrics: coalescing ratio, batch sizes
- No regression in capture freshness for active panes

## Estimated Effort
2-3 hours implementation, 1 hour testing

## Benchmark Requirements
- **criterion benchmarks** with target `benches/notification_coalescing.rs`:
  - `coalescing_throughput`: measure on_notification() call throughput under burst load (10,000 notifications in 100ms for 50 panes). Target: >1M notifications/sec ingestion rate.
  - `drain_ready_latency`: measure time to scan pending map and produce the ready set. Target: <100us for 50-pane pending map.
  - `coalescing_ratio`: measure the reduction factor — ratio of input notifications to output capture operations under burst workload. Target: >20:1 ratio for active panes.

## Property-Based Testing (proptest)
- **No-loss invariant**: for any sequence of on_notification() calls, every pane_id that received at least one notification is eventually returned by drain_ready() within max_delay_ms. No event is silently dropped.
- **Coalescing correctness**: multiple notifications for the same pane_id within a single window produce exactly one drain_ready() entry for that pane. No duplicate captures.
- **Timing bound**: for any notification, the time between on_notification() and the corresponding drain_ready() return is always >= window_ms and <= max_delay_ms.
- **Independence**: notifications for different pane_ids do not interfere with each other's timing. Pane A's coalescing window is independent of pane B's notifications.

## Concurrency Testing (LabRuntime DPOR)
- **LabRuntime with DPOR exploration** for concurrent event producers:
  - Multiple tasks call on_notification() simultaneously for the same and different pane_ids. DPOR explores all interleavings to verify that no notification is lost, the pending DashMap is consistent, and drain_ready() returns each pane_id at most once per window.
  - Concurrent drain_ready() and on_notification(): DPOR verifies that a notification arriving during drain_ready() is either included in the current drain or deferred to the next window (never lost).

## Lock-Free Event Queue (Loom verification)
- **Loom model checking** for the lock-free event queue alternative:
  - If DashMap is replaced with a lock-free concurrent data structure (e.g., crossbeam SegQueue + AtomicU64 timestamps), use Loom to exhaustively verify that the lock-free implementation is linearizable: every on_notification() is visible to a subsequent drain_ready(), and no ABA problems occur on the timestamp tracking.
  - Loom verification of the DashMap retain() + insert() interaction: verify that retain() during concurrent insert() never causes a notification to be permanently lost (DashMap's shard-level locking should guarantee this, but Loom confirms it under all interleavings).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:36:10.058256Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T22:18:55.020088-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-x4rq","depends_on_id":"wa-3cyp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:08:12.168714Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"wa-xedi","title":"TUI workflow progress panel: active workflows with step-by-step progress bars and logs","description":"
# TUI Workflow Progress Panel

## Purpose
Provide real-time visibility into workflow execution with step-by-step progress visualization.

## UI Design
```
│ Workflows                                                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ▶ handle_usage_limits (Pane 9)  Step 3/7: Selecting account...      │
│   [████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 42%                                         │
│ ▶ handle_compaction (Pane 3)    Step 2/3: Injecting context...      │
│   [██████████████░░░░░░] 67%                                         │
```

## Features
1. Show all active workflows with current step name
2. Progress bar based on step count
3. Expand to see full step log
4. Time elapsed and estimated remaining
5. Cancel/pause buttons for stuck workflows
6. Color coding: green=success, yellow=running, red=failed

## Implementation
- Subscribe to workflow_step_log events
- Track step count and current step per workflow
- Calculate progress percentage from step index
- Provide expand action to show step details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Active workflows appear with progress bars
- [ ] Step names and numbers update in real-time
- [ ] Expand shows full step history
- [ ] Failed steps are highlighted red
- [ ] Cancel action stops workflow gracefully

## Testing
- Manual smoke test: panel renders with fixture workflow data.

 {"id":"wa-xfgv","title":"FTUI-09.3.a Compile-time guardrails against ratatui reintroduction","description":"## Background
Migration completion is fragile without guardrails that prevent ratatui/crossterm regressions from re-entering changed paths.

## Deliverables
- compile-time/static checks that fail when forbidden imports appear in migration-complete modules
- allowlist/exception mechanism with explicit expiry rationale
- developer guidance for resolving violations and selecting ftui-native alternatives

## Acceptance Criteria
- guardrails run in CI and locally with deterministic pass/fail output
- violation logs clearly identify file/module and remediation path
- policy prevents silent backsliding while allowing narrowly scoped, documented exceptions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:44.745714205Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.216674-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:34:40.9487521Z"}
 {"id":"wa-xkcj","title":"Pane bookmarks + aliases","description":"## What
Allow users to bookmark panes with friendly names and optional tags.

## Why
Operators need fast access to critical panes without guessing IDs.

## How
- Storage for bookmarks (pane_id, alias, tags)
- CLI: `wa panes bookmark add/list/remove`
- Filters by alias/tag in status/search
- Add storage migrations via wa-y6g if new tables are required

## Success Criteria
- Bookmarks persist across restarts
- Alias collisions are handled clearly","notes":"2026-02-08: Added missing bookmark alias/tag filtering to wa status and wa search in crates/wa/src/main.rs, including multi-pane tag search merge behavior and CLI parser tests (cli_search_parses_bookmark_filters, cli_status_parses_bookmark_filters).
